content: According to the Vayu Purana [1] and the Narada-Purana [2], the Puranas describe four yugas (Krta, Treta, Dvapara, and Kali) with specific durations and characteristics. Each yuga has a distinct impact on human life and society, with Krta being an age of equality and piety, and subsequent yugas experiencing a decline in dharma and an increase in adharma. The yugas are also associated with changes in the color of Lord Narayana and the stability of dharma, with each yuga having a shorter span of life and increased distress.
